metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the grep sweep term from `land_contract` (underscore) to `land-contract` (hyphen) so the grep-clean AC actually catches all dangling references in README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md, and docs/README.md.
findings.finding: The grep sweep pattern 'land_contract' (underscore) is the wrong identifier and will miss every live dangling reference to the deleted 'docs/land-contract.md'. All actual references in the codebase use the hyphenated form 'land-contract' (README.md:299, CONTRIBUTING.md:161, CLAUDE.md:595, docs/README.md:108, docs/adr/0042-auto-lander.md). After deletion these become broken links, but the grep-clean AC passes trivially on the underscore term, giving a false-negative result and allowing the CI suite to fail later (docs index test, link checker).
findings.location: What section — grep sweep list: 'autolander, land_contract, land-status'; AC line 1 'No references to infra/autolander or the land command remain (grep clean)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `land_contract` with `land-contract` in the What grep sweep list and AC1. The full sweep command would be: `grep -r 'land-contract\|land_contract\|autolander\|land-status' . --include='*.md' --include='*.py' --include='*.sh' --include='*.yml' --include='*.yaml' --include='*.tf' | grep -v '.rebar/cache'`

findings.finding: The grep sweep pattern listed in the What section is 'land_contract' (underscore), but every actual reference to the deleted document in the repo uses 'land-contract' (hyphen) — in README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md, docs/README.md, docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md, and elsewhere. A grep for 'land_contract' returns zero hits and will silently pass even when broken doc links to the deleted docs/land-contract.md remain, undermining the plan's own sweep-based verification mechanism.
findings.location: What section — 'grep sweep: autolander, land_contract, land-status'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the grep sweep term to 'land-contract' (hyphen) to match the actual artifact name. Since broad doc updates are scoped to story 5e7a, it is acceptable to note that grep-for-'land-contract' hits in top-level contributor docs (README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md) are tracked under 5e7a and intentionally left for that story — but the sweep term must use the correct form so the implementer can make that judgment consciously rather than missing the hits entirely.

findings.finding: The plan's grep sweep term `land_contract` (underscore) does not match the actual identifier used in all live references, which use `land-contract` (hyphen). A sweep using only the underscore form will miss references in README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md, docs/README.md, docs/adr/, and infra/autolander/land.py.
findings.location: What section: 'grep sweep: autolander, land_contract, land-status'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the What bullet to: 'grep sweep: autolander, land-contract, land-status'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the grep sweep to include the hyphenated form `land-contract` (and `land contract`) to catch markdown links and prose references to the deleted `docs/land-contract.md`.
findings.finding: The grep sweep terms listed (`autolander`, `land_contract`, `land-status`) use underscore/exact forms but omit the hyphenated form `land-contract`, which is the exact filename of the deleted doc and the likely link text in README, CONTRIBUTING, or other markdown files. References of the form `[land-contract](docs/land-contract.md)` or `docs/land-contract.md` would not be caught, leaving stale doc links after deletion.
findings.location: What section — grep sweep: 'autolander, land_contract, land-status'; also AC 'No references to infra/autolander or the land command remain (grep clean)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `land-contract` and `land contract` to the grep sweep command, e.g.: `grep -r 'autolander\|land_contract\|land-status\|land-contract\|land contract' --include='*.md' --include='*.py' --include='*.toml' --include='*.cfg' .`

findings.finding: AC1 states 'No references to infra/autolander or the land command remain (grep clean)' — but after deleting only the code scope the plan describes, confirmed-existing references in infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf, infra/terraform/ssm.tf, infra/nginx/rebar.conf.template, infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh, infra/scripts/compose-up.sh, infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh, infra/scripts/observability.sh, infra/gerrit/smoke-check-autolander.sh, infra/gerrit/project.config, and docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md would still be present. ADR 0047 scopes these to separate work items (bcf0, 5e7a), but the AC as written makes no such exclusion and would evaluate as failing.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'No references to infra/autolander or the land command remain (grep clean)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC1 with: 'No references to infra/autolander/, tests/autolander/, or the land/land-status commands remain in src/, pyproject.toml, or any file not covered by sibling work items bcf0 (infra decommission) and 5e7a (docs sweep).'

findings.finding: The grep sweep terms listed are 'autolander, land_contract, land-status' (underscore form), but the deleted doc is named 'land-contract.md' (hyphen). Other documentation files (READMEs, CHANGELOG, contribution guides, wiki pages) that reference the hyphenated forms 'land-contract' or 'land command' could be missed by the sweep, stranding stale references.
findings.location: What section — 'Remove any imports/registry entries/refs to the above (grep sweep: autolander, land_contract, land-status)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the grep sweep: `grep -r 'land.contract\|land.status\|land command\|autolander' docs/ *.md` (or equivalent) alongside the code sweep, and confirm zero hits before closing.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the grep sweep term from `land_contract` (underscore) to `land-contract` (hyphen) to match the actual identifier used throughout the repo (README.md:299, CONTRIBUTING.md:161, CLAUDE.md:595, docs/README.md:108, etc.).
findings.finding: The plan's grep sweep term is written as `land_contract` (underscore), but every actual reference to the contract document in the repo uses the hyphenated form `land-contract` — confirmed at README.md:299, CONTRIBUTING.md:161, CONTRIBUTING.md:537, CLAUDE.md:595, docs/README.md:108, docs/adr/0042-auto-lander.md:11 and :77, docs/adr/0047-retire-autolander-rebase-if-necessary.md:127 and :131. The underscore form appears only inside cached plan-review JSON artifacts. A sweep on `land_contract` will match none of these live references and produce a false-clean result.
findings.location: What section — 'grep sweep: autolander, land_contract, land-status'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the sweep pattern to `land-contract` (hyphen). Also add explicit update steps for README.md:299, CONTRIBUTING.md:161 and :537, CLAUDE.md:595, and docs/README.md:108, which all link to docs/land-contract.md and must be updated or have the link removed when the file is deleted.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the prose 'grep clean' AC with an explicit anchored command, e.g. `grep -rn --include='*.py' --include='*.md' -E '\bautolander\b|\bland_contract\b|\bland-status\b|\bland command\b' . && echo FAIL || echo CLEAN`, scoped to the repo root.
findings.finding: The grep-clean AC names search terms (autolander, land_contract, land-status) but does not specify a concrete anchored grep command, leaving the proving command susceptible to substring false-positives and unclear directory scope.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'No references to infra/autolander or the land command remain (grep clean)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Change the grep sweep term from `land_contract` (underscore) to `land-contract` (hyphen) to match every actual reference in the repo.
findings.finding: The plan's grep sweep term 'land_contract' (underscore) will not match any real reference in the repo — every occurrence uses the hyphenated form 'land-contract' (the filename is docs/land-contract.md). AC1's grep-clean check would silently pass while live references remain in README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md, docs/README.md, and docs/adr/ files.
findings.location: What section — grep sweep: 'autolander, land_contract, land-status'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `land_contract` with `land-contract` in the grep sweep list: `grep -r 'autolander\|land-contract\|land-status' .`

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Update the module-size allowlist / any config referencing infra/autolander' — but probing `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt` and `.github/module-size-ceilings.txt` confirms that NO infra/autolander file is listed in either file (both files contain only `src/rebar/` paths). The update-allowlist work item rests on an unverifiable premise.
findings.location: What section — 'Update the module-size allowlist / any config referencing infra/autolander'

findings.finding: Deleting docs/land-contract.md without also updating docs/README.md (which links to it at line 108) will break the `test_current_repo_index_is_clean` test via scripts/check_docs_index.py's broken-link checker, causing `make test` to fail.
findings.location: What section / AC: 'make lint && make typecheck && make test green'; docs/README.md line 108
findings.suggested_fix: In docs/README.md, remove or update the `- **[land-contract.md](land-contract.md)** — ...` bullet (line 108) as part of this ticket.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out plan (e.g., 'revert commit X' or a feature-flag gate) and confirm/document that no external consumers (CI, operator scripts, other repos) invoke `land` or `land-status` before merging.
findings.finding: The plan performs an irreversible code deletion but provides no explicit rollback/back-out path, and does not acknowledge whether any external consumers (CI pipelines, operator scripts, other repos) invoke the `land` or `land-status` CLI commands.
findings.location: ## What / ## Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Add a short 'Rollback' section: 'This deletion is reversible by reverting the merge commit. Pre-merge: confirm no external CI or operator scripts reference `land`/`land-status` by running the grep sweep against infra configs and pipeline definitions.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update AC1 to explicitly name both `land` and `land-status` commands (e.g., 'No references to infra/autolander, the land command, or land-status remain').
findings.finding: AC1 checks for 'the land command' but the What section describes two distinct CLI entities — `land` and `land-status` — and the grep sweep terms list 'land-status' separately; AC1's phrasing 'the land command' may not cover `land-status` as a distinct verified entity.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — AC1 vs ## What bullet 2
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC1 to: 'No references to infra/autolander, the land command, land-status, or land-contract remain (grep clean for: autolander, land_contract, land-status, land command).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit statement (or a one-line check) confirming there are no external consumers of the `land`/`land-status` commands or `land-contract.md` (e.g., cross-repo grep, team confirmation), or note how any such consumers will be notified/redirected.
findings.finding: The plan does not explicitly acknowledge whether external callers (other repos, downstream CI pipelines, published CLI documentation, or users of the `land`/`land-status` commands) depend on the deleted behavior. The plan states the bot is 'being dropped' but does not confirm that no external consumers exist or how they are handled.
findings.location: ## What — Remove the land / land-status CLI command + its wiring, and docs/land-contract.md
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line under ## Why or ## What such as: 'Confirmed no external repos or users invoke the land/land-status commands (cross-repo grep result: zero hits); docs/land-contract.md has no inbound links. No migration action required.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit deletion/update steps for the infra artifacts that contain live autolander references but are not named in the What section: infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf, infra/terraform/ssm.tf (autolander-gerrit-token param), infra/compose/Dockerfile.autolander, infra/compose/docker-compose.yml (autolander service + volumes), infra/nginx/rebar.conf.template (/autolander/status block), infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh, infra/scripts/observability.sh, infra/scripts/compose-up.sh, infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh, and infra/gerrit/smoke-check-autolander.sh.
findings.finding: The plan names only infra/autolander/, tests/autolander/, the CLI command wiring, and docs/land-contract.md as deletion/update targets, then relies on a grep sweep to catch remaining references. However, grep confirms live autolander references in at least nine files OUTSIDE these named targets: infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf (CloudWatch alarm resource), infra/terraform/ssm.tf (SSM param for autolander-gerrit-token), infra/compose/Dockerfile.autolander (entire Dockerfile), infra/compose/docker-compose.yml (autolander service + volumes), infra/nginx/rebar.conf.template (/autolander/status proxy), infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh (AUTOLANDER_SERVICE, build logic), infra/scripts/observability.sh (AUTOLANDER_CONTAINER metric emission), infra/scripts/compose-up.sh (SITE_SUBDIRS), infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh (autolander-gerrit-token), and infra/gerrit/smoke-check-autolander.sh (entire file). None of these are named as targets in the What section. AC1 ('no references remain, grep clean') will fail unless all these are also cleaned.
findings.location: What section (named deletion targets) and AC1 ('No references to infra/autolander or the land command remain (grep clean)')
findings.suggested_fix: Add a fifth What bullet: 'Delete/clean infra/compose/Dockerfile.autolander; remove the autolander service and volumes from infra/compose/docker-compose.yml; remove the /autolander/status location from infra/nginx/rebar.conf.template; remove the AUTOLANDER_* blocks from infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh, observability.sh, compose-up.sh, and fetch-secrets.sh; delete infra/gerrit/smoke-check-autolander.sh; delete infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf and remove the autolander SSM param from infra/terraform/ssm.tf.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add explicit deletion/editing targets for the 10 unenumerated infra/ consumer files: `infra/compose/Dockerfile.autolander` (delete), `infra/compose/docker-compose.yml` (remove autolander service + volumes), `infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf` (delete), `infra/terraform/ssm.tf` (remove autolander-gerrit-token row), `infra/nginx/rebar.conf.template` (remove /autolander/status proxy block), `infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh` (remove AUTOLANDER_* vars + deploy logic), `infra/scripts/compose-up.sh` (remove autolander SITE_SUBDIRS entries), `infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh` (remove autolander-gerrit-token fetch), `infra/scripts/observability.sh` (remove autolander error-metric block), `infra/gerrit/smoke-check-autolander.sh` (delete).
findings.finding: Numerous infra/ operational files outside infra/autolander/ contain autolander references that must be edited or deleted but are not enumerated in the plan: infra/compose/Dockerfile.autolander (whole file), infra/compose/docker-compose.yml (autolander service + two named volumes), infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf (whole file), infra/terraform/ssm.tf (autolander-gerrit-token SSM param row), infra/nginx/rebar.conf.template (/autolander/status proxy block), infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh (AUTOLANDER_* vars + deploy logic), infra/scripts/compose-up.sh (autolander SITE_SUBDIRS entries), infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh (autolander-gerrit-token fetch), infra/scripts/observability.sh (autolander error-metric scrape), infra/gerrit/smoke-check-autolander.sh (whole file).
findings.location: What section — 'Remove any imports/registry entries/refs to the above (grep sweep)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add README.md (line 299), CONTRIBUTING.md (lines 161, 537), CLAUDE.md (line 595), and docs/README.md (line 108) as explicit edit targets to remove/update their references to docs/land-contract.md and the `land` command.
findings.finding: Three top-level documentation files that link to docs/land-contract.md are not enumerated as edit targets: README.md (line 299 links to docs/land-contract.md and references the 'land' command), CONTRIBUTING.md (lines 161 and 537 link to docs/land-contract.md), and CLAUDE.md (line 595 links to docs/land-contract.md). Additionally docs/README.md (line 108) indexes land-contract.md by name — deleting the file without updating docs/README.md will leave a broken docs index link.
findings.location: What section — 'Remove the land / land-status CLI command + its wiring, and docs/land-contract.md'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly carve out infra/compose (Dockerfile.autolander, docker-compose.yml), infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh, and infra/terraform/*autolander* from the leaf's 'any config' sweep and grep-clean AC, noting these belong to the decommission-infra child — or confirm with the parent epic owner that decommission-infra files are in scope here.
findings.finding: The leaf's catch-all 'Update the module-size allowlist / any config referencing infra/autolander' and its grep sweep term 'autolander' are broad enough to overlap with files the parent epic explicitly assigns to the separate decommission-infra child (infra/compose/Dockerfile.autolander, infra/compose/docker-compose.yml autolander service, infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh AUTOLANDER_PATHS, infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf, infra/terraform/ssm.tf). The leaf does not carve out those paths, so it may inadvertently pre-empt the decommission-infra sibling or create a double-deletion conflict.
findings.location: What section — 'Update the module-size allowlist / any config referencing infra/autolander' and 'grep sweep: autolander'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a scoping note under the What section: 'Scope excludes infra/compose/Dockerfile.autolander, infra/compose/docker-compose.yml (autolander service stanza), infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh, and infra/terraform/*autolander* — those are handled by the decommission-infra child.' Update AC1 to match: 'No references to infra/autolander in src/, tests/, pyproject.toml, or the module-size allowlist remain (grep clean within those paths).'

findings.finding: AC1 states 'No references to infra/autolander or the land command remain' but the plan does not touch numerous files with such references that are deferred to later tickets (infra/compose/docker-compose.yml, infra/compose/Dockerfile.autolander, infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh, infra/scripts/compose-up.sh, infra/scripts/fetch-secrets.sh, infra/scripts/observability.sh, infra/terraform/monitoring_autolander.tf, infra/terraform/ssm.tf, infra/nginx/rebar.conf.template, README.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md). As written, AC1 will not be satisfiable after just this ticket's changes.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'No references to infra/autolander or the land command remain (grep clean)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical to AC1: '(infra/compose, infra/scripts, infra/terraform, nginx, and prose-doc references are cleaned in follow-on tickets bcf0 and 5e7a per ADR-0047 §Migration)'

findings.finding: AC3 ('Obsoletes bugs dc33, 15a1, 0d3e — close them when this lands') is listed as an Acceptance Criterion but describes a one-time administrative transition (closing tickets) that is not verifiable in-session via repo artifacts or CI, and is not tagged [operator-attested] per ADR 0043.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 ('Obsoletes bugs dc33, 15a1, 0d3e (close them when this lands)')
findings.suggested_fix:
Change AC3 to:
`- [ ] [operator-attested] Close bugs dc33, 15a1, 0d3e — record close-event IDs/timestamps as attestation on this ticket when the PR lands.`

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
